programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects Gibbs's arch-nemesis is behind an explosion at a summit on global terror. Meanwhile, a grief-stricken Fornell struggles to deal with the fatal shooting of his ex-wife. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Joe Spano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A covert identity created for one of Gibbs' undercover cases 20 years previously resurfaces after a murder victim is found to be using the alias. Meanwhile, Bishop becomes anxious at the thought of having to work incognito in the future. Starring Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy lieutenant is murdered at the Defence Logistics Agency, while visitings the office of an admiral currently away at sea. As the team investigates the scene of the crime, they realise a rare painting found in the admiral's office is actually an elaborate fake placed there after the killing, replacing the stolen original. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. Luckily, Tony's con-artist father has returned to town, having broken up with his fiancee, and is on hand to provide expert advice as the hunt for the killer takes them into the shady world of the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

In the first episode, her husband Joe gives descriptions of her dreams to law enforcement agencies across the country - and one of the images bears a resemblance to a homicide in Texas. However, she comes into conflict with a cynical ranger who is sceptical about her ability

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Club owner Iain Andain looks certain to escape rape and assault charges for the third time in his seedy career when Sam agrees to drop charges in return for information about a major drug dealer. Callum is determined the unsavoury lowlife won't get away with it yet again and encourages his indignant young constables Will and Ben to dish out their own brand of justice

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A woman reports seeing a body being thrown into the river and a search recovers the remains of a hospital doctor recently exonerated from charges of professional negligence after losing a patient on the operating table. Crime Scene Examiner Eddie Olosunde discovers that the man had been suffering from MS and had argued with a colleague about concealing his plight from the General Medical Council

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton upsets everyone by deciding to join his PCs on the beat, which couldn't come at a worse time for Beth and Emma, who are suffering after a night's drinking. Fortunately, Gina and Nikki trail them and step in when an arrest has to be made at Canley Market. Roger and Nate find a delivery driver beaten up by a truck-jacker who has made off with a consignment of electrical goods

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sally is rescued from a nightclub brawl by an undercover officer from another station, only to learn he was the instigator of the disturbance and in possession of cocaine. When he appeals to her to cover for him she makes a split-second decision that puts her career on the line. Meanwhile, a victim of the fight reveals an obsessive desire to catch up with the dealer who sold drugs to his daughter

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is flattered by the attentions of charming Dr Hunter, at least on first impressions, but soon changes her opinion when his housekeeper dies suddenly

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor's absence is sorely felt, leaving Robert to take on a young woman who hopes to be a medical student, although Dr Marsham is far from impressed

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor discovers a darker side to village life when there is a measles outbreak at the local school

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is rocked by a series of violent attacks on women, and a prostitute is too ashamed to report what she knows - but her actions lead to a dangerous man roaming the streets, with devastating consequences for the residents of Nonnatus House. A mother tries to keep her daughter's pregnancy a secret to avoid gossip, putting her life in danger. The Turners go on a long overdue holiday, but the weather ruins their plans

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The introduction of the contraceptive pill is welcomed by Dr Turner, but some of his colleagues are less keen on the idea. Patsy tries to help a bargee who wants to give birth among her own community rather than in a maternity ward. Nurse Crane deals with an emergency during a storm, and a familiar face returns to Nonnatus House - but has been changed by her time away

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is horrified to realise he may be partly to blame as the cause of the recent rise in birth deformities comes to light, and tries to prevent further cases. Sister Evangelina returns to work, but has clearly been affected badly by her stroke, and her health continues to deteriorate. A pregnant bride goes into labour during her wedding reception, while her mother is forced to come to terms with her grandchild being conceived out of wedlock

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Christmas special of the period drama, which catches up with the nurses and midwives of Nonnatus House in 1962. They receive a letter from a doctor running a mission clinic on the Eastern Cape of South Africa, who is in desperate need of their help to save her urgently needed hospital from closure. The nurses head to Africa to lend a hand, finding the clinic understaffed, underfunded and with a poor water supply, but overwhelmed with patients who need their help more than anyone ever has before. Sinead Cusack guest stars

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives return home after their trip to South Africa, to find Nonnatus House has come under new management. The formidable Sister Ursula (Harriet Walter) has taken charge and introduced some drastic changes to the way the clinic is run, which Sister Julienne, bound by her vow of obedience, is powerless to oppose. The midwives come to the aid of a victim of domestic violence whose husband is about to be released from prison, and who risks losing everything if she seeks a divorce. Sister Mary Cynthia suffers further anxiety attacks, and Patsy receives distressing news about her father

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki is humiliated in court when a barrister accuses her of making a serious error in the case of a policeman who may or may not have been murdered. Meanwhile, the Lintern team investigates the death of a medical researcher, whose body was found in a canal with mysterious puncture marks on it. Long-running crime drama following the cases of a team of forensic pathology experts, starring David Caves, Emilia Fox, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr, with Art Malik and Dervla Kirwan

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: The opening of Hugh's new cardiac clinic is upset by life-changing news from his ex-wife, and Charlie's life is thrown into disarray when her father makes a visit

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Meryl and Carlito's relationship leaves the Knight family reeling, the tension between Hugh and Penny endangers the new cardiac clinic, and Matt reconnects with a high school crush

A sceptical Paul has more pressing matters on his mind in the form of financial worries and the imminent departure of Iona. Ewan, however, is quick to cash in on the commercial possibilities as scientists arrive to investigate the creature. Susan Hampshire and Lloyd Owen star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy answers a call about a pensioner who has been attacked during a burglary, so Stevie and Mickey set up a sting operation that reveals a well-established outfit selling stolen goods on the Internet. When two hoodies previously seen arguing with the victim have been eliminated from the inquiry, the officers are left with some unlikely suspects, reminding them how deceptive appearances can be

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Faye announces she does not plan to return to Highfields with baby Harry, prompting Joseph to step in and invite her to spend Christmas with him. Meanwhile, Penny lets power go to her head when she is asked to cover registrar responsibilities, and life as a single working mother starts to get the better of Chrissie - but things begin to look a little brighter when Sacha offers her a shoulder to cry on

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry wants to do the right thing by the baby and decides to propose to Ellen. On the opening day of the hepatitis B clinic, Maggie berates a woman for allowing her daughter to work as a prostitute. Sam is forced to put his dreams of becoming a paramedic on hold, prompting him to consider resigning

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Beth gets a lead on a drug dealer and volunteers to go undercover to collect more evidence. Max reluctantly agrees and the plucky PC finds herself at a party where she strikes up a friendship with DJ Lenny Jones (David Ajala) but has to leave in a hurry when she spots a man holding a gun. An arrest follows, but loose ends mean she must play her role once more, putting herself into even more danger

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy has last minute reservations about her cancer operation but George persuades her to go ahead. Alan is now living with Frankie, but has some explaining to do to Bianca

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy is back on the hospital ward following her operation for breast cancer. Grant and Tiffany have row at the 70s night about her drinking while pregnant

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The lads are bemused after they are ordered to capture a dangerous escaped panther, and wonder if they are the victims of a hoax. Meanwhile, Evans is disturbed to learn a friend may be bullying an Asian soldier

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Helen returns after attending her aunt's funeral, but James has a number of calls on his time, including dealing with complications arising during the birth of a calf. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Part two of two. The roguish antiques dealer finds a Scottish gothic temple brings him closer to Lady Jane, while love is in the air for Eric. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hestor is on edge as unmarried daughter Emma announces she's expecting a baby and news reaches her that a food critic will be attending Lucy's kitchen during her shift. Julia McKenzie stars

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom decides to go on hunger strike at the Bayview Retirement Village until the standard of food improves. First episode of the sitcom, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Suspicious about the validity of Foggy's war stories, Compo and Clegg use an old school challenge to establish the truth. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Marina wants to deliver a giant cuddly panda as her birthday present to lover Howard, so ropes in the reluctant trio to help. Comedy, featuring a guest appearance by comedian Jim Bowen and starring Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The first of two final cases for the detective, played by Martin Shaw. Gently is on the brink of retirement when he, Bacchus and Coles re-open the case of a woman convicted of killing her husband, hoping to uncover a long-buried miscarriage of justice. However, the investigation places Gently's career on the line and stirs up tension with Bacchus, who worked on the original investigation

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jack turns out to have a personal connection to the prime suspect in a teenage girl's murder, so he is taken off the case and replaced with another forensic scientist seconded to the Lyell Centre. But he finds himself instinctively drawn into the investigation, alienating himself from Nikki and the team as he tries to find an alibi for the supposed killer. Meanwhile, the victim's best friend is consumed with guilt, leading her to take drastic action. Crime drama, starring David Caves, Emilia Fox and Richard Lintern, with guest appearances by Haydn Gwynne and Ashley Walters

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Beth gets a lead on a drug dealer and volunteers to go undercover to collect more evidence. Max reluctantly agrees and the plucky PC finds herself at a party where she strikes up a friendship with DJ Lenny Jones (David Ajala) but has to leave in a hurry when she spots a man holding a gun. An arrest follows, but loose ends mean she must play her role once more, putting herself into even more danger

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The 12th-century sleuth sets out to clear his novice Oswin of murdering a nun whose body has been found frozen in a block of ice. While tracking down the culprit, he is also forced to confront the fact he fathered a son before he took holy orders. Medieval mystery based on the novels of Ellis Peters, starring Derek Jacobi, Mark Charnock, Eoin McCarthy and Julian Firth

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Malcolm arrives to deal with the forest's blight problem - and Paul is dismayed to discover the newcomer is Iona's ex-fiance. Molly coaches Jess to compete in the ghillies' gruelling annual challenge, despite the fact the other competitors snub her efforts. Meanwhile, Ewan is envious when Amy starts spending a great deal of time with her art tutor. Highland drama, starring Lloyd Owen and Rae Hendrie

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Beth gets a lead on a drug dealer and volunteers to go undercover to collect more evidence. Max reluctantly agrees and the plucky PC finds herself at a party where she strikes up a friendship with DJ Lenny Jones (David Ajala) but has to leave in a hurry when she spots a man holding a gun. An arrest follows, but loose ends mean she must play her role once more, putting herself into even more danger

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: It is Christmas Day, but Connie fails to feel the festive cheer as her father is rushed in for emergency surgery. Meanwhile, a rare opportunity for a heart-and-lung transplant patient is thrown into jeopardy, and Faye is forced to make the most difficult decision of her life after Jac thwarts her plot to leave England with baby Harry

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Selena considers resigning as police surgeon after failing to section an unstable woman who goes on to endanger the lives of those around her. Charlie distributes leaflets in the red-light district to help the failing hepatitis B clinic, but is arrested after being set up by a disapproving pimp

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ben discovers the body of drug addict Abbey Ferguson and the trail of evidence leads back to her pimp Danny Peters, who witnesses claim lashed out when she refused to sleep with a client who was known to be violent. Stevie and Stuart set out to arrest the brothel keeper, but the encounter takes a horrifying turn when the suspect brandishes a dirty heroin syringe

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lorraine learns a shocking secret about Grants paternity of Tiffany's baby. Simon agrees to let Dan stay. Peggy comes home from hospital

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy returns from hospital after her cancer operation. Sanjay hangs mistletoe on the stall but takes it down when Gita kisses the male customers

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: An old flame from Northern Ireland turns up on Sergeant McLeod's doorstep with his six-year-old son in tow - a development his wife is not too pleased about. Jeremy has an embarrassing date with Melanie, and soon realises he has more in common with her mother - but she is married to his commanding officer

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Christmas in Skeldale brings no respite from animal emergencies, as pampered pooch Tricky Woo falls ill and Mrs Pumphrey entrusts him to Helen's care. Christopher Timothy and Carol Drinkwater star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A dawn raid by bailiffs leaves the antiques dealer staring ruin in the face, and with nothing left to look forward to, he decides to turn his back on society. With Ian McShane and guest star Alexei Sayle

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William organises a special getaway to mark their 24th wedding anniversary. He surprises Hester by taking her back to the hotel where he first proposed - but an even bigger surprise is waiting for them when they get there

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The staff at Bayview Retirement Village are sent into a panic when Diana and Tom head off to Brighton for the day in a borrowed Porshe without telling anyone. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Desperate to earn some cash, Compo reveals a musical talent after being endorsed by several artists in the area and sets out as a one-man band. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Brian Wilde and Peter Sallis

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy finds an interest in camcorders, much to the annoyance of some country ramblers. When later playing his footage back on Pearl's video machine, it becomes apparent the lens has inadvertently caught her and Howard in a compromising situation. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When the body of a woman is found, all evidence points to suicide - but Havers is adamant foul play has occurred. Reinstated to the rank of Detective Sergeant and paired once more with Lynley, she sets out to uncover the mysteries surrounding the victim. The trail leads the pair to an exclusive club where Havers goes undercover to delve beneath the respectable surface and find the killer. Jenny Agutter guest stars, with Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back in 1983, 16-year-old Amy Taskerland was murdered on the night of the school disco. More than 30 years later, the unearthing of a time capsule and the discovery of her voice on a mix tape inside prompts Miller and co to delve back into the case, starting by interviewing Amy's best friend Harriet and her former teacher Mr Hines. The recording features a strange mix of music and poetry, including words from a speech written in secret for the Queen for broadcast in the event of a nuclear war. But how did Amy get a copy of it, and was its secret nature the reason she was killed? Elsewhere, Miller enjoys her first romance since her divorce, while Gerry goes on his future son-in-law's stag night. Amy Nuttall (Emmerdale, Downton Abbey) and Jack Shepherd (Wycliffe) guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki and Jack are called to a petrol station where three people have been shot dead, and conclude it is the work of a concealed shooter working from long range - a sniper is at large in London. They combine ballistics and wound pathology with the killer-profiling skills of senior investigator DCI Jane De Freitas, as they work to find this seemingly random murderer before panic hits the city. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern, with Zoe Telford and Steve Wall

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ben discovers the body of drug addict Abbey Ferguson and the trail of evidence leads back to her pimp Danny Peters, who witnesses claim lashed out when she refused to sleep with a client who was known to be violent. Stevie and Stuart set out to arrest the brothel keeper, but the encounter takes a horrifying turn when the suspect brandishes a dirty heroin syringe

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: When the medieval monk investigates the murder of a wealthy cleric, the finger of suspicion immediately points to a new arrival at the abbey, but as Cadfael digs deeper, he uncovers connections to a family with tangled loyalties. programme.title: Welsh Whisperer - Ni'n Teithio Nawr
programme.desc: Cameras follow country folk pop performer Andrew Walton, aka the Welsh Whisperer, on his journey through rural villages of Wales, meeting characters who are integral to their local communities and village life. In this episode, he travels north to Sarn Meyllteyrn

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Chelsea v West Bromwich Albion. The Foxes and the Blues were looking for three points to boost their chances of finishing in the top four, but Leicester faced the toughest possible test against the champions elect. Plus, Arsenal v Liverpool and Leeds United v Sheffield United

programme.title: Fern Britton's Holy Land Journey
programme.desc: The presenter travels to Jerusalem on a life-changing journey of faith, in search of the real story of Jesus' final days, from his triumphal arrival, to his brutal crucifixion

programme.title: Easter Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Most Rev Justin Welby shares his Easter message of hope and resurrection, in a joyful celebration, live from the stunning setting of Canterbury Cathedral. Following Covid-safe guidelines, members of Canterbury Cathedral Choir, directed by David Newsholme, sing well-loved hymns and anthems including Jesus Christ is Risen Today, This Joyful Eastertide and Thine be the Glory. The Mass setting is Mozart's Missa Brevis in B Flat. With Gospel music from Junior Garr and members of the Spirituals Choir and readings and prayers from the Rt Rev Dr Rose Hudson-Wilkin, Bishop of Dover, and members of the Community of St Anselm

programme.title: Urbi et Orbi
programme.desc: Pope Francis gives his Easter Message and Blessing on the ninth Easter of his pontificate

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alexander and Arit Anderson visit gardens expressing hope, joy and renewal. At the Bishop's Palace in Wells, Somerset, Alexander discovers why this place has been sacred for centuries, and helps to create a colourful and uplifting work of art. Arit learns how the ancient trees at Scone Palace in Perthshire will be given a new lease of life, and Alexander heads to his home county of Northumberland to tour a garden using humour to plant seeds of change in the community

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help two teams buy items at Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits the Museum of Timekeeping in Newark

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins celebrates Easter Sunday from Llandaff Cathedral in Cardiff. She finds out about the meaning behind the Paschal Candle, while James Lusted meets a vicar who has turned his life around. Featuring special performances from Bryn Terfel, Llandaff Cathedral Choir, and Vocal Creation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of teachers with a £600,000 budget who want to upsize and find the ideal country home for them and their four young children. Away from property, Jonnie turns the heat up on his Lancastrian tour when he visits one of the last working forges in the county

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: The Boat Race
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the men's and women's University Boat Races between Oxford and Cambridge, relocated this year to the River Great Ouse in Cambridgeshire. The race was cancelled last year and it was Cambridge who prevailed in both races in 2019 at the traditional venue of the River Thames, with two-time Olympic champion James Cracknell among the members of their men's team, becoming the oldest competitor in the event's history at the age of 46. With analysis from Cracknell, Matthew Pinsent and Katherine Grainger, and commentary by Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents the show from the Golden Triangle in Gloucestershire, an area famous for wild daffodils. She talks to local people who remember picking the wild flowers as children and visits some of the finest medieval wall paintings in England. Charlotte Smith investigates the challenges facing rural churches and the communities they serve, and a mother and son reveal how they have fulfilled their dreams of farming in the Cumbrian hills

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Culzean Castle in South West Scotland, and uncovers the story of a former slave who established himself at the heart of the local community. Treasures brought in to be valued include a 1914 FA cup medal, a valuable Art Nouveau clock, and a huge statue found in a compost heap

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for a middle-aged gentleman. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: On the strength of evidence from a new witness, Jo brings Terry Boyle back in for questioning about Gail Vella's murder. Kate is torn between loyalty to Jo and wanting to assist AC-12's investigation - and tensions only increase when she encounters a face from the past with links to organised crime

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Newcastle United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion. Spurs and the Red Devils were looking to boost their chances of qualifying for the Champions League with victories against sides battling against relegation. Plus, Southampton v Burnley, Aston Villa v Fulham and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the latest Super League fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Chelsea v Birmingham City

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts the property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house, while a celebrity panel try work out which one is telling the truth. This week, regulars Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix are joined by Rob Rinder to find the real Michael. From the first sight of the four players the panel are instantly thrown by a familiar face, while the path to the truth is obscured by another Michael's inability to remember anything about his own life

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A married couple from Merthyr Tydfil answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin visit properties in Bilston near Wolverhampton, Nettlestead Green in Kent and Etruria in Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: An elderly woman has her handbag snatched on her way to bingo - but the robber doesn't get far. Police follow a digital trail of evidence and put him behind bars

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A man stuck in the mud at a building site in Larkhall tests the skills of Special Operations Paramedics and firefighters who need to pull him out without harming his legs and feet

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Join Christina Trevanion for today's Bargain Hunt in Leominster in Herefordshire. Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri are helping the teams, and Christina learns about tribal art

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuth must stop a much-loved institution from going off the rails when the local stationmaster is murdered. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple with a budget of £650,000 find a home in rural Somerset, and also visits the apple orchards of Barrington Court and samples some traditional cider

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Two sisters from Northern Ireland provide today's ingredients for Akis Petretzikis and Ellis Barrie, hoping the chefs will give them some ideas on dishes they can recreate at home. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a vintage nursing chair, a helmet belonging to a firefighter involved in the Aberfan disaster, and a traditional Italian coffee grinder

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particular struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon is feeling daunted about her new venture, while Jean's desperation lands her in hot water. Ruby is struggling to cope looking after Martin and Stacey's kids, and Kush is none to happy about Arthur living with her. Sheree and Isaac clash over the best course of action for Patrick, and Iqra is horrified to hear that the picture from her photo shoot has been posted online

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: The rumour that hotel inspectors are in town brings out the grovelling worst in Basil as he tries to spot the undercover enemy without upsetting all the guests. Comedy, guest starring Bernard Cribbins, with John Cleese and Prunella Scales

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the first episode of finals week, in which the five remaining contenders must cook a five-course lunch at the City of London's Plaisterers Hall for members of the hospitality sector who delivered thousands of meals to frontline workers during the first lockdown. The contestants must each serve up as dish of their own creation, inspired by their own lockdown experience, then return to the studio to make culinary tributes to their heroes

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Faryal receives makeup samples, and Amir gives the Bolton lads a wedding hall tour. Business calls in Mayfair as they commission a glitzy piece of art

programme.title: Meet the Khans: Big in Bolton
programme.desc: Amir opens up about how not fighting has impacted his mental health. Faryal receives a shocking business proposal and seeks support after an influx of trolling abuse online

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for a middle-aged gentleman. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Basic Instinct and Casino star Sharon Stone, who talks about her memoir The Beauty of Living Twice. Hollywood greats Octavia Spencer and Melissa McCarthy discuss working together in superhero comedy Thunder Force, while David Schwimmer and Nick Mohammed - co-stars of spy series Intelligence - also appear. Comedian Frank Skinner and singer and star of RuPaul's Drag Race Michelle Visage are also on Graham's guest roster. Plus, Steps perform Heartbreak in This City

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A return visit to a detached house in Ashford, Kent, that Lucy Alexander first saw back in 2013. Dion Dublin explores a ground-floor flat in Birmingham and Martel Maxwell looks round a two-bedroom terrace in Stoke with no kitchen or bathroom

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: The search for ram raiders who targeted a jewellers in Northampton - and how automatic number plate recognition cameras eventually saw them put them behind bars

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paramedic crews battle treacherous conditions to transfer a patient for a life-saving operation, assist the coastguard in Fife and are called to a non-responsive patient

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help teams at Southwell Racecourse. Host Eric Knowles oversees proceedings, and also visits the Lincoln Vintage Vehicle Society

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne calls on help to exercise creative flair to an outdated armchair, while blacksmiths Bex and Dave are tasked with turning old scaffolding props into something special

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a young family to escape the city and find some space and tranquillity in the Hampshire countryside with their £800,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Good friends Heather and Kelly provide today's ingredients for Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, hoping the chefs will give them some ideas on dishes they can recreate at home. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers work on a pre-war metal pedal plane that has been grounded for decades, a ukulele fashioned from an old cigar box and a collection of badly broken toy zoo animals

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Labour Party candidate for the London mayoral election

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon gives Jean a chance to talk through her family concerns, although when Sharon mentions her new-found brother, Jean is left with an idea. Martin is caught in the middle as Lily and Ruby argue, and Whitney realises Kush is worried about leaving Arthur. Isaac reminds his mother Sheree of her controlling tendencies as she refuses to allow Patrick take part in the medical trial

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The truth is out about Reyhan's abuse, although Sahira is adamant that her father is innocent, leaving Hanssen feeling compromised. Louis discovers old friend Vicky is caught in a toxic relationship and vows to help. But who can he turn to? Kian tries to convince Lucky that his drug taking is a thing of the past

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Nurse Chloe starts her first shift at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, while a toddler arrives having difficulty breathing

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: Keeley and the syndicate tell Mercury Millions they are the real lottery winners and that Frank has stolen their money. Without their ticket, however, they have no proof - so they realise their only option is to track him down in Monaco themselves. As they scrape together the money for their flights, Frank is living the high life - but his outrageous gambling is attracting attention all over the city. Meanwhile, a customer asks Colette out for a drink, leaving her worried that if she gets close to him, he might learn the truth about her past

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary following how British Muslims mark some of life's biggest moments. Married couple She and Ali look back at their vows and reflect on how their faith has helped them in their marriage. Emine marks the death of her mother with a final prayer, symbolising her mum coming from god, and going back to god

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Roman Catholics Maria and Peter have their fifth child baptised in a traditional Latin service and in Stockton-on-Tees family and friends pay their last respects to June, one of the first women to be ordained as a priest by the Church of the England in 1994

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma's brother Caleb puts in an appearance, and she gets an ally in her campaign to prevent the bypass. Meanwhile, a girl captures Norman's interest. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan wonders where his loyalties lie and Norma is initiated into society by Christine. Meanwhile, Norman is less than happy about having Caleb around. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley discovers how science is helping better understand nature on Bedfordshire's Greensand Ridge, where he reports on the world's longest running bird survey, helps to map the levels of plastic pollution, and joins the Toad Patrol creating a new national amphibian DNA database. Tom Heap investigates the UK's environmental ambitions, and Adam Henson provides a spring update on his farm

programme.title: Easter from King's
programme.desc: Music and readings for Holy Week with the choristers of the Choir of King's College, Cambridge, and the King's Singers, directed by Daniel Hyde. Well-loved choral music for the season includes Ubi Caritas, God So Loved the World, When I Survey the Wondrous Cross and This Joyful Eastertide, while the story of Jesus' death and Resurrection is told in the words of the King James Bible, read by members of King's College

programme.title: Britain's Easter Story
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson learn how Easter was once banned and reveal the story of a hymn by prolific writer Charles Wesley. With music from the Kingdom Choir

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler tries to become self-sufficient in fruit and vegetables as she uses her small back garden to plant, grow and harvest her own produce. She begins with peas and beans, and provides useful growing tips as well as recipes including broad-bean falafels and pea-shoot cocktails. She also forages for willow to make plant supports and buys two chickens

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The team returns to Beechgrove's home turf and after a year away from the garden. Carole and Brian set up neighbouring patches for some healthy competition, while Head Gardener Mairi reclaims Beechgrove after its time on furlough in 2020. George is back chopping down trees in his home garden in Joppa, while Kirsty builds a stylish greenhouse on her brand-new ornamental allotment

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With delicious dishes celebrating Scotland's landmark inventions, who will be judged the winner for Scotland? The judges, including bestselling author Caroline Criado-Perez, must decide. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Mary Berry's Easter Feast
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mary explores the foods that bring different communities together on Easter Sunday, the most symbolic and meaningful feast in the Christian calendar. As well as sharing her own family favourites, Mary discovers how the Greek Orthodox community breaks the fast with tsoureki bread, spends a day with Archbishop of York John Sentamu and discovers Filipino and Italian Easter specialities. She also puts on a feast at her local church hall for everyone she has met along the way

programme.title: Easter Parade (1948)
programme.desc: A dancer abandoned by his usual partner trains a chorus girl as a replacement. The new pairing is a great success but, although he strives to keep their relationship strictly professional, he begins to fall head over heels in love with her. Musical, with Fred Astaire, Judy Garland, Peter Lawford and Ann Miller. Songs include A Couple of Swells, Stepping Out with My Baby and Shaking the Blues Away

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane explores the wealth of wildlife attracted to the Firth of Forth. Tessa Dunlop reveals how the Victorian zeal for cleanliness turned the Thames into a giant toilet bowl

programme.title: The Vikings (1958)
programme.desc: A Norse warrior becomes embroiled in a feud with a former slave over the succession to the Northumbrian throne, unaware his arch-enemy is really his half-brother. Matters come to a head when they both set their sights on a Welsh princess, leading to a confrontation - but the situation is further complicated by the interference of England's sadistic king. Adventure, with Kirk Douglas, Tony Curtis, Janet Leigh, Ernest Borgnine and James Donald

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Another host of leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. In this opening episode, Nadiya Hussain takes Fred to visit some of her beloved eateries around Yorkshire. Whilst on their journey, the pair sample traditional bakes, world-class cheese and spicy samosas

programme.title: The Real T-Rex with Chris Packham
programme.desc: Chris Packham embarks on a global journey to discover the truth about the tyrannosaurus rex, which has endured centuries of scientific inaccuracy and Hollywood misrepresentation. The naturalist uses new studies into dinosaur skin, teeth and muscles, as well as groundbreaking insights into its behaviour to create the most accurate CGI representation of the dinosaur ever produced. To help, Chris speaks to experts in birds, biomechanics and alligators, and has access to one of the most complete tyrannosaurus rex skeletons in existence

programme.title: The Day the Dinosaurs Died
programme.desc: Documentary examining whether an asteroid strike 66 million years ago triggered a worldwide catastrophe, killing off the dinosaurs and wiping out 75 per cent of all life on Earth. Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod joins a multimillion pound drilling expedition into the exact spot in the Gulf of Mexico that was the point of impact, while palaeopathologist Alice Roberts meets leading scientists to find if the effects could have wiped out dinosaurs across the world almost immediately

programme.title: Brazil with Michael Palin
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey across the South American country, Michael explores the Brazilian south, where he is surprised by the rich diversity of European and Asian influences. Along the way he meets Dom Joao de Orleans e Braganca, second in line of succession to Brazil's defunct throne, goes flying with a man who has made a fortune out of rubbish and meets rap star Criolo, who believes social equality is a distant dream for most of his countrymen. Travelling farther south to Blumenau, the former Python's views on what makes a typical Brazilian are challenged when he finds German speakers and Bavarian dancers, before he catches piranha for sushi and helps cowboys treat a calf attacked by a jaguar

programme.title: Eye of the Storm
programme.desc: Renowned landscape painter James Morrison faces his greatest challenge - his eyesight is fading fast, and he has one more major painting to complete. Filmmaker Anthony Baxter follows him as he picks up the brushes again at the age of 85 after being sidelined by a series of operations, and while doing so, he reflects on an extraordinary artistic life

programme.title: Red Joan (2018)
programme.desc: An elderly widow is arrested for treason, and shares the story of her life as the KGB's longest serving agent in the British government. Recruited as an idealistic radical in the 1930s, she spent decades providing the Soviet Union with details of Britain's nuclear weapons - in the hope that neither nation would gain the edge in the arms race. Espionage drama based on Jennie Rooney's novel, starring Judi Dench, Sophie Cookson and Nina Sosanya

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter uses his new-found ability to help Emma accept her new life and Sylar steps up his attempts to gain full control over Matt's body. Meanwhile, Noah intervenes as Claire tries to discover who is behind the attacks on Gretchen and Annie - only to realise he may be somehow responsible

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt continues his battle to resist Sylar's mental domination, and Tracy increasingly begins to lose control of her abilities. Meanwhile, Samuel learns the full extent of his powers and takes dangerous measures to ensure he achieves maximum potential

programme.title: Black Power: A British Story of Resistance
programme.desc: A look at the 1960s and '70s through the eyes of the young black people who refused to take racism as a part of the natural order of things in the UK. The documentary shows how the civil rights struggle in America inspired activists to take a stand, and features rare archive of Martin Luther King, Malcolm X and Stokely Carmichael's activities in Britain, as well as footage of leading figures in the movement in the UK including Altheia Jones-LeCointe, Darcus Howe and Roy Sawh

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Cameron's comeuppance, Nicky vents her frustrations on a patient - and is forced to ask herself whether she really wants to raise a killer's baby. Reyhan challenges a traumatised Hanssen to tell Sahira the truth, but can he do it knowing it will destroy her? The AAU staff are surprised to see Ange back in scrubs so soon after her ordeal

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It's the Easter weekend, which means extra time to enjoy the garden. Monty Don plants out sweet peas for summer colour and gives advice on what to use for mulching borders. Plus, Carol Klein is at RHS Rosemoor to revel in the floral spring sensations that epitomise the season

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the brutal attack of a 96-year-old D-Day war veteran on his doorstep, with police in Taunton, quickly arresting a suspect after tracking his spending habits

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: The giant pandas keep everyone on their toes and monkey Dennis gets hurt in a fight. A tiny insect is at the heart of one conservation project, beavers at the core of another

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates how spicy food works well with a cool accompaniment, preparing a chicken and chilli curry, spiced rice pudding and hot and spicy prawns

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough has a passion for birds' eggs, which protect the young from the outside world at the same time as allowing them to breathe and are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. In this documentary, the broadcaster reveals the secrets of birds' eggs from creation to hatching, exploring how they are made, the reasons behind their shape and their intrinsic purpose

programme.title: The Fall of the Roman Empire (1964)
programme.desc: Roman emperor Marcus Aurelius is poisoned by the henchmen of his mad son Commodus because he intends to hand power to the boy's more favoured and able adopted brother. But lacking sanity and authority, the usurper loses military control and Rome is overrun with barbarians. Historical drama, starring Alec Guinness, Christopher Plummer, James Mason, Sophia Loren, Anthony Quayle and Omar Sharif

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best finds made on the series, when experts including Michael Baggott, Anita Manning, Thomas Plant, Mark Stacey, David Harper and Catherine Southon located a gilded cherub and a collection of Victorian prints among many other items of interest. Plus, while in the grounds of Beaulieu Abbey in Hampshire, presenter Paul Martin recalls the story of the dissolution of the monasteries

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A much-loved childhood bike and a wartime Royal Navy kit bag are among the items transformed by the skilled team of crafters into new and useful items for their owners

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary and her husband celebrate their 50th wedding anniversary, and she shares her secrets on how to create surprisingly easy showstoppers, including canapes inspired by the 1960s

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the first semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of the Booker Prize, the city of York, the Busby Babes, and the Russo-Japanese War, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the second semi-final of the quiz, with four more returning contenders answering questions on the specialist subjects of films based on the works of Stephen King, Joan Rivers, US state capitals, and Neil Gaiman novels.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as two teams battle it out in the grand final of the student quiz, endeavouring to succeed last year's winners Imperial College London and become the 50th champions, carrying off the trophy designed by Manchester sculptor Adrian Moakes

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary America's Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Highlander (1986)
programme.desc: A 16th-century Scottish clansman discovers he is one of a race of immortal warriors who can only be killed by beheading, and who are destined to fight to the death until just one survives. Centuries later in modern-day New York, he prepares for a final showdown with his deadliest enemy. Fantasy action adventure, starring Christopher Lambert, Sean Connery, Clancy Brown and Roxanne Hart. Featuring a soundtrack by Queen

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley discovers how science is helping better understand nature on Bedfordshire's Greensand Ridge, where he reports on the world's longest running bird survey, helps to map the levels of plastic pollution, and joins the Toad Patrol creating a new national amphibian DNA database. Tom Heap investigates the UK's environmental ambitions, and Adam Henson provides a spring update on his farm

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste meet a woman whose online friend tried to gain her trust and affection to fleece her out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: A trip to Devon sees Simon Parfett and his team help Lucy and Rob, who are setting up their perfect B&B overlooking the Teign Valley in a '70s Californian airstream

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: An elderly woman has her handbag snatched on her way to bingo - but the robber doesn't get far. Police follow a digital trail of evidence and put him behind bars

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes the most of the clocks going forward as he begins to sow dahlia seeds for summer flowers. He also reveals a new project, and shows what to plant now in the vegetable garden. Adam Frost extends the planting in his gravel garden and reveals his plans for the coming year

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a shipping company manager, a teacher and a singer must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights and analysis from the second round of matches in the Super League season, including St Helens v Hull Kingston Rovers, Wigan Warriors v Wakefield Trinity, Hull FC v Salford Red Devils and Leeds Rhinos v Castleford Tigers

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: With George and Smiler along for the ride, Woody makes his secret trip to Australia. However, there is a shocking surprise in store there for him. Back in Weld, Shay remains the object of Ollie's unwanted affections. Comedy drama, with Rick Donald

programme.title: Nature's Great Migrations
programme.desc: Documentary following a thousand elephants as they come together in a crucial gathering in northern Kenya, while facing threats from both predators and poachers. Liz Bonnin and a team of scientists and filmmakers use cutting-edge tracking technology to follow individual elephants as they cope with daily challenges and make the most of this unique, short-lived gathering. They also capture surprising new behaviour and witness how the elephant society responds to poaching, including the way in which young elephants cope with the loss of their parents

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring some of the world's most famous ecosystems continues, this time focusing on the Serengeti, the east African grassland that has become home to some of the greatest concentrations of herbivores on the continent. Steve Backshall explains how this landscape developed into such a diverse and densely populated habitat

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques at Birmingham's Museum and Art Gallery, where experts Thomas Plant and Christina Trevanion examine items brought in by members of the public. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits Hagley Hall in Worcestershire, the last great Palladian house to be built in Britain

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: A beloved gramophone, which reminds the owner of her music-loving grandad, and a old slide projector belonging to a photography enthusiast are among today's transformations

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: A couple living in a Bungalow near Brighton are treated to two virtual reality visions for what their home could become by architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison. Silvia and Julian bought the property because they loved the garden, and thought they could make something of the house - but their renovations have not been a success. Robert proposes a way to make the planning rules that have restricted them work for the project, while Laura finds a way to bring the beloved garden inside the house. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Another host of leading food experts take Fred Sirieix to their favourite restaurants to explore what makes them so special. In this edition, Chris Bavin takes Fred on a culinary venture around the New Forest, where fresh local produce is king, featuring wood-fired cooking, pork pies and a country pub serving a special Ploughman's

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti explore one of the world's most luxurious eco resorts on the small island of Tetiaroa in French Polynesia. Surrounded by a coral reef and an abundance of marine life, they work alongside staff of the exclusive Brando Resort to serve five-star luxury to guests paying up to £11,000 per a night

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Man Like Mobeen star Guz Khan hosts the stand-up showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. On the bill are Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Rhys James

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Pandemic 2020
programme.desc: The story of the first year of the pandemic told by people from all over the world. It begins on New Year's Eve 2019, when huge crowds gathered to celebrate. Contributors include Xie, a young food blogger from Wuhan whose wedding took place a few days before her city went into lockdown. As the virus travels to Europe, Dr Amie from Coventry recalls watching in disbelief the horrific scenes of people dying in Italy

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace present the last of the heats which sees five more hopefuls competing for an apron by cooking their signature dish - something that tells John and Gregg about who they are as a cook and how good they can become. The two best will automatically go through, while the remaining three must cook two courses for John and Gregg as well as Dhruv Baker and Alex Rushmer - champion and finalist from the 2010 competition

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett travels to the Blackdown Hills on the Somerset-Devon border, where he turns a 1960s trailer into a funky festival-themed accommodation

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Eggs: The Genius of Carl Faberge
programme.desc: Stephen Smith explores the life and work of the renowned jeweller, best known for the eggs he made out of precious metals and gemstones for the last tsars of Russia. The presenter talks to Prince Michael of Kent about Faberge items in the Royal Collection, and to Russian oligarch Viktor Vekselberg, who spent $100million acquiring nine of the trinkets. He also follows in Faberge's footsteps, from the Green Vaults in Dresden to the corridors of the Kremlin museum, to discover how a modest family business was turned into the world's most famous supplier of luxury items

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker reminisce about their all-time favourite sketches, including Garlic Breath Party, Sid and George - It's a Duck, Mrs Mace Case, Swedish Made Simple, Inventors' Convention and a Come Dancing musical number. Alison Moyet provides a break from the comic mayhem with a performance of Windmills of Your Mind

programme.title: The Two Ronnies: The Studio Recordings
programme.desc: A collection of unedited takes from the classic comedy show, featuring both famous and more obscure sketches from programmes recorded in 1979 and 1980. There is also footage of Ronnie Barker and Ronnie Corbett entertaining the studio audience during breaks in filming one of their celebrated newsdesk sketches

programme.title: Talking Comedy

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Clark investigates a sexual harassment case involving accusations of bribery, and turns up widespread prejudice within the force. Gritty drama, starring Neil Pearson

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: An influential officer obstructs Clark's investigation into an attack on a prostitute, but that does not stop his quest for justice and a promotion

programme.title: Between the Lines

programme.title: Sacred Songs - The Secrets of Our Hearts
programme.desc: British vocal ensemble Tenebrae give a special performance of music for Easter, with all 20 of its singers filmed and recorded separately, within their enforced isolation at home, under the direction of Nigel Short. The repertoire includes Bach: Wenn ich einmal so scheiden, Lobo: Versa est in luctum, Allegri: Miserere, Purcell/Croft: Thou knowest, Lord, the secrets of our hearts, Parry: My soul, there is a country and Bach: Ach Herr, lass dein lieb engelein

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook

programme.desc: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed on the Shropshire Union Canal, before accidentally flooding a staircase lock in Chester

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a quiet mountain masterpiece with a beautiful lake in a glen that will leave you rested and at peace with the world.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould must prove that a sketchbook is the work of a young Toulouse-Lautrec, overturning a decision made by a committee of experts on the artist. The drawings in the book are of a very different subject matter to Lautrec's famous works, and are dated to when he was a teenager - a period of his life that is largely a mystery. To get to the truth, Fiona and Philip travel to the town in the south of France where Lautrec grew up and visit the grand family home where he spent much of his youth

programme.title: The $50m Art Swindle
programme.desc: Documentary telling the remarkable story of a charlatan art dealer who swindled more than $50million from the art establishment before going on the run. Michel Cohen, a popular and charming New York art dealer originally from France, was a high-school drop-out from a poor background who went on to become a rich and successful art dealer. Throughout the 1990s, he sold paintings by artists such as Picasso, Monet and Chagall to America's wealthiest elite. Cohen was living the high life until he began trading recklessly in the stock market and ran up considerable debts. In an attempt to recoup his losses, he swindled private collectors, auction houses and other art dealers out of more than $50million. When his crimes were discovered, he fled the US with his wife and two children and went on the run. In 2003, he was found by Interpol in Brazil and was imprisoned in Rio de Janeiro, but seven months later while awaiting extradition to the US, he escaped from prison and vanished off the face o

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how art in France took a dramatic turn after the Revolution ushered in a bold new world of artists including Jacques-Louis David and Theodore Gericault

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary recalling events that occurred in 1964, when large groups of young people who identified with two disparate subcultures descended upon seaside resorts on motorbikes and scooters - sparking violent confrontations. The programme considers how their repeated clashes created widespread moral panic about the state of British youth, the notion of which was undergoing a vast transformation due to greater prosperity and freedom

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their extraordinary consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. The second episode focuses on the story of the murder of Julie Hogg, a single mother from the small town of Billingham in Teesside. The programme looks back at Julie's mother Ann's campaign to challenge the 800-year-old law on Double Jeopardy, after a jury failed to convict Julie's former boyfriend Billy Dunlop. As this episode traces the twists and turns of Ann's campaign, the story of other cases that benefited from the legal changes are also explored

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary examining the history of capital punishment, including the protocol of public execution and the science of hanging. Archive information reveals the offences that resulted in the gallows and how children as young as seven were hanged

programme.title: Lights Up Festival
programme.desc: Jazmin is the only mixed-race woman in her small west country village and suffers racism on a regular basis. She longs to break free of the bullies and move to the big city to become an actress. Semi-autobiographical drama, written by and starring Natasha Marshall

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Ashwika is passionate about protecting India’s only ape: the hoolock gibbon. Spending most of their lives in the canopy and rarely coming to ground means their populations are hugely threatened by deforestation, with their population decreasing by 90 per cent in just 30 years. Four years ago, Ashwika travelled to Assam and met a gibbon that touched her heart, and from that moment on she has wanted to protect them. On this mission she is going to a village where the people live peacefully alongside the gibbons and have done for many generations, and learns about why they care about them so much. Along the wa,y she witnesses habitat loss with her own ‘eye in the sky’. And she meets one of India’s top gibbon scientists, who is doing everything he can to save the species with the help of local villagers.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Plastic is one of the greatest inventions in human history. It makes so much of our lives possible, but how we dispose of it is now a massive problem. By the year 2050 it’s thought that there will be more plastic than fish in our seas. So what can we do about it? First, ditch the disposable plastic water bottle and go for tap water in a reusable bottle. Better for the environment and cheaper too! Next, join a beach clean. It may seem like this problem is too big for us to handle but many hands make light work. Reduce, reuse and, importantly, recycle. And even more importantly than that, don’t drop litter. A bag that is thrown on the streets of London could end up on the coast of Africa or in the belly of a whale or a turtle. Lastly and most importantly, know that every single person can make a difference. One single plastic bag could choke a turtle or sunfish. One plastic straw could be the end of a gorgeous, iconic animal, so know you have power and use it.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. It’s Safari Worlds annual recycling event, and Andy has been busy tidying up and encouraging the guests to recycle their litter. Mr Hammond calls as he is worried that the new clownfish are not settling in okay after their long trip from Borneo. They are leaving the safety of their sea anemone home, which they hardly ever do in the wild. Mr Hammond is at a loss as to why they are doing this, but Andy reassures him that they will get to the bottom of it. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Uploading his wet suit, aqua pack and gizmo, Andy runs to the Aqua Dome’s loading dock and boards his safari-sub, a hi-tech underwater submersible capable of travelling to any location on the planet in the blink of an eye. Next, Hannah puts pieces of her salmon onto individual baking sheets and spoons the marinade on. She garnishes them with freshly chopped spring onions, and then her salmon is ready to be cooked in the oven. Hannah wears a colourful Korean outfit and treats her friends to some local Korean kimchi, but will her pals be able to guess her special ingredient?

programme.desc: Join 10-month-old Teddy and Ada as they discover the world around them and learn to communicate through signs and words. Ada and Teddy are both at home playing with toy phones in the garden. Teddy’s Mum takes him to a BSL class where they play with bubbles to help them learn some signs. Ada goes to a signing class and learns the signs for “more” and “milk”. Teddy then visits a butterfly house and gets to see some reptiles and meerkats! After lunch, Teddy’s siblings show him how to use a shape sorting toy, and Ada visits the park to play a game of find the tennis ball. Finally, Teddy practices the signs he learned with his family. programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: It’s a big day for Grace the Great Tit as the brand new bird feeder cafe is opening for business. She’s spent weeks getting everything ready, and it’s all looking perfect. That is, until Maisie the Grey Squirrel comes and spills seeds everywhere. All of Grace’s hard work is ruined, and all her customers have been scared off. How on earth is she going to get her lovely cafe open again?

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to the place they can do their recycling. On the way they climb a lookout tower, listen to the wind blowing in the trees, play musical statues and even beep like reversing buses!

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour silver, like silver-looking zips, and explore objects that appear silver-coloured in towns and cities. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour silver.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Cognitive development series for toddlers using non-verbal, non-narrative imagery, music and games. Explore a blue swing and a disco in the era of the Renaissance, and watch as Book Boy watches television by doing magic.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 11-month-old Rudi and Everley as they discover the world around them and see what they do to relieve their teething pain. Rudi and Everley begin their day eating breakfast with their families. Everley and her family are in the garden, looking at each other’s teeth in a mirror and counting how many they have. Rudi then plays with a teething mitten to soothe his teeth, before having fresh fruit. Everley visits her family dentist, where the dentist checks her teeth and talks to mum about the importance of teething. Once home, Everley’s mum gives Everley her very own toothbrush! Mum brushes her own teeth and shows Everley how to brush her teeth. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: A diplodocus skeleton at a museum provides a scare for Tig - but Woolly explains it is nothing to be worried about

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Zahra-Rose's family is from Somalia, a country situated on the east coast of Africa. In Somalia, they have the traditional dish of nafaqo, which is boiled egg wrapped in mashed potato. This is what Zahra-Rose has chosen to make for her friends. To begin with, Zahra-Rose seasons mashed potato with salt, black pepper, flour, ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder and fresh coriander. She then wraps the potato mixture around her special ingredient, hard-boiled eggs, rolls them in breadcrumbs and puts them in the oven to be cooked. Zahra-Rose also serves a fresh chilli sauce, so her friends can use it for dipping. Not only does Zahra-Rose have beautiful decorations, she also has a surprise picnic planned. Will her friends realise what her special ingredient is?

programme.title: CBeebies Presents
programme.desc: Adaptation of Lewis Carroll's classic tale in which a young girl learns to use her imagination as she journeys through a fairy-tale land. Staged at the Wales Millennium Centre, Cardiff, with a cast featuring numerous CBeebies stars, including Justin Fletcher, Ben Faulks, Katrina Bryan, Phil Gallagher and Pui Fan Lee

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘lemon’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see a yellow lemon, a leafy lemon, a juicy lemon and a sour lemon. We meet a freezing lemon, a lazy lemon, a loud lemon and even a loop-the-loop lemon! The journey is repeated with some surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!
