programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Chelsea v West Bromwich Albion. The Foxes and the Blues were looking for three points to boost their chances of finishing in the top four, but Leicester faced the toughest possible test against the champions elect. Plus, Arsenal v Liverpool and Leeds United v Sheffield United

programme.title: Fern Britton's Holy Land Journey
programme.desc: The presenter travels to Jerusalem on a life-changing journey of faith, in search of the real story of Jesus' final days, from his triumphal arrival, to his brutal crucifixion

programme.title: Easter Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Most Rev Justin Welby shares his Easter message of hope and resurrection, in a joyful celebration, live from the stunning setting of Canterbury Cathedral. Following Covid-safe guidelines, members of Canterbury Cathedral Choir, directed by David Newsholme, sing well-loved hymns and anthems including Jesus Christ is Risen Today, This Joyful Eastertide and Thine be the Glory. The Mass setting is Mozart's Missa Brevis in B Flat. With Gospel music from Junior Garr and members of the Spirituals Choir and readings and prayers from the Rt Rev Dr Rose Hudson-Wilkin, Bishop of Dover, and members of the Community of St Anselm

programme.title: Urbi et Orbi
programme.desc: Pope Francis gives his Easter Message and Blessing on the ninth Easter of his pontificate

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alexander and Arit Anderson visit gardens expressing hope, joy and renewal. At the Bishop's Palace in Wells, Somerset, Alexander discovers why this place has been sacred for centuries, and helps to create a colourful and uplifting work of art. Arit learns how the ancient trees at Scone Palace in Perthshire will be given a new lease of life, and Alexander heads to his home county of Northumberland to tour a garden using humour to plant seeds of change in the community

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help two teams buy items at Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits the Museum of Timekeeping in Newark

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins celebrates Easter Sunday from Llandaff Cathedral in Cardiff. She finds out about the meaning behind the Paschal Candle, while James Lusted meets a vicar who has turned his life around. Featuring special performances from Bryn Terfel, Llandaff Cathedral Choir, and Vocal Creation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of teachers with a £600,000 budget who want to upsize and find the ideal country home for them and their four young children. Away from property, Jonnie turns the heat up on his Lancastrian tour when he visits one of the last working forges in the county

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: The Boat Race
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the men's and women's University Boat Races between Oxford and Cambridge, relocated this year to the River Great Ouse in Cambridgeshire. The race was cancelled last year and it was Cambridge who prevailed in both races in 2019 at the traditional venue of the River Thames, with two-time Olympic champion James Cracknell among the members of their men's team, becoming the oldest competitor in the event's history at the age of 46. With analysis from Cracknell, Matthew Pinsent and Katherine Grainger, and commentary by Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents the show from the Golden Triangle in Gloucestershire, an area famous for wild daffodils. She talks to local people who remember picking the wild flowers as children and visits some of the finest medieval wall paintings in England. Charlotte Smith investigates the challenges facing rural churches and the communities they serve, and a mother and son reveal how they have fulfilled their dreams of farming in the Cumbrian hills

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Culzean Castle in South West Scotland, and uncovers the story of a former slave who established himself at the heart of the local community. Treasures brought in to be valued include a 1914 FA cup medal, a valuable Art Nouveau clock, and a huge statue found in a compost heap

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for a middle-aged gentleman. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: On the strength of evidence from a new witness, Jo brings Terry Boyle back in for questioning about Gail Vella's murder. Kate is torn between loyalty to Jo and wanting to assist AC-12's investigation - and tensions only increase when she encounters a face from the past with links to organised crime

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Newcastle United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion. Plus, Southampton v Burnley, Aston Villa v Fulham and a round-up of yesterday's goals

This week, regulars Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix are joined by Rob Rinder to find the real Michael. From the first sight of the four players the panel are instantly thrown by a familiar face, while the path to the truth is obscured by another Michael's inability to remember anything about his own life

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particular struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: Keeley and the syndicate tell Mercury Millions they are the real lottery winners and that Frank has stolen their money. programme.title: The Boat Race
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the men's and women's University Boat Races between Oxford and Cambridge, relocated this year to the River Great Ouse in Cambridgeshire. The race was cancelled last year and it was Cambridge who prevailed in both races in 2019 at the traditional venue of the River Thames, with two-time Olympic champion James Cracknell among the members of their men's team, becoming the oldest competitor in the event's history at the age of 46. programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particular struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A return visit to a detached house in Ashford, Kent, that Lucy Alexander first saw back in 2013. Dion Dublin explores a ground-floor flat in Birmingham and Martel Maxwell looks round a two-bedroom terrace in Stoke with no kitchen or bathroom

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: The search for ram raiders who targeted a jewellers in Northampton - and how automatic number plate recognition cameras eventually saw them put them behind bars

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paramedic crews battle treacherous conditions to transfer a patient for a life-saving operation, assist the coastguard in Fife and are called to a non-responsive patient

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help teams at Southwell Racecourse. Host Eric Knowles oversees proceedings, and also visits the Lincoln Vintage Vehicle Society

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne calls on help to exercise creative flair to an outdated armchair, while blacksmiths Bex and Dave are tasked with turning old scaffolding props into something special

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a young family to escape the city and find some space and tranquillity in the Hampshire countryside with their £800,000 budget

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Good friends Heather and Kelly provide today's ingredients for Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill, hoping the chefs will give them some ideas on dishes they can recreate at home. Hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers work on a pre-war metal pedal plane that has been grounded for decades, a ukulele fashioned from an old cigar box and a collection of badly broken toy zoo animals

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon gives Jean a chance to talk through her family concerns, although when Sharon mentions her new-found brother, Jean is left with an idea. Martin is caught in the middle as Lily and Ruby argue, and Whitney realises Kush is worried about leaving Arthur. Isaac reminds his mother Sheree of her controlling tendencies as she refuses to allow Patrick take part in the medical trial

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The truth is out about Reyhan's abuse, although Sahira is adamant that her father is innocent, leaving Hanssen feeling compromised. Louis discovers old friend Vicky is caught in a toxic relationship and vows to help. But who can he turn to? Kian tries to convince Lucky that his drug taking is a thing of the past

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Nurse Chloe starts her first shift at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, while a toddler arrives having difficulty breathing

programme.title: The Syndicate
programme.desc: Keeley and the syndicate tell Mercury Millions they are the real lottery winners and that Frank has stolen their money. Without their ticket, however, they have no proof - so they realise their only option is to track him down in Monaco themselves. As they scrape together the money for their flights, Frank is living the high life - but his outrageous gambling is attracting attention all over the city. Meanwhile, a customer asks Colette out for a drink, leaving her worried that if she gets close to him, he might learn the truth about her past

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the Belfast Community Gospel Choir's preparation for its annual winter concert, exploring the stories of members of the group. The group has been singing for 10 years, adding a specifically Northern Irish flair to traditional gospel and soul music. Here, members of the group tell their stories and reveal their reasons for joining

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary following how British Muslims mark some of life's biggest moments. Married couple She and Ali look back at their vows and reflect on how their faith has helped them in their marriage. Emine marks the death of her mother with a final prayer, symbolising her mum coming from god, and going back to god

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Roman Catholics Maria and Peter have their fifth child baptised in a traditional Latin service and in Stockton-on-Tees family and friends pay their last respects to June, one of the first women to be ordained as a priest by the Church of the England in 1994

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norma's brother Caleb puts in an appearance, and she gets an ally in her campaign to prevent the bypass. Meanwhile, a girl captures Norman's interest. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Dylan wonders where his loyalties lie and Norma is initiated into society by Christine. Meanwhile, Norman is less than happy about having Caleb around. A prequel to Alfred Hitchcock's Psycho, starring Vera Farmiga, Freddie Highmore and Max Thieriot

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a session of the DCMS Committee on the economics of music streaming, with witnesses from Amazon, Apple and Spotify, as well as DCMS minister Caroline Dinenage, from Tuesday 23 February. First shown on BBC Parliament on 27 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Recorded coverage

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: MPs on the Health and Social Care Committee hear from NHS England Chief Executive Sir Simon Stevens and NHS England Chief Operating Officer Amanda Pritchard, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: Highlights of the week in politics in the USA from C-SPAN

programme.title: Washington Journal
programme.desc: Highlights of the week from the USA

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of the work of committees set up to investigate aspects of public policy and society

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk: Stephen King Stephen Sackur interviews prolific writer Stephen King. He's written more than 60 books and sold close to 400 million copies. Films like the Shining, Carrie and Stand by Me are all based on his stories. He's been writing horror for half a century, but how has our appetite for fear evolved?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Health and Social Care select committee session on the Health and Social Care White Paper with evidence from the Health Secretary, Matt Hancock, and Jason Yiannikkou, Director of NHS Legislation Programme at Department of Health and Social Care

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint National Security Strategy Committee session on the UK's national security machinery, with evidence from former prime minister David Cameron and former national security advisor Peter Rickets, from Monday 1 March.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Despite losing the presidency and both Houses of Congress, Donald Trump still seems to have a chokehold on the Republican party. So what should Republican anti-Trumpers do now? Do they fight from within, or create a new Conservative Party? Stephen Sackur interviews Evan McMullin, one of their key organisers and strategists

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Former prime minister Tony Blair speaking at the Institute for Government on the last twelve months of the Covid-19 pandemic, its lasting impact and the challenges posed for politicians, from Friday 26 February

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: David Cornock tells the story of how Toynbee Hall and London's East End shaped the politics of Labour prime minister Clement Attlee

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump's presidency is history. So not surprisingly historians are trying to find some perspective on a president who stress-tested American democracy like no other. Stephen Sackur speaks to Timothy Snyder, one of America's leading historians on totalitarianism and the Holocaust. He believes Trump and his movement brought America face to face with early-stage fascism, but is that pushing historical parallels too far? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 22 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The debate in the House of Lords on the 2021 Budget, from earlier today.

programme.title: Parliamentary Firsts
programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a short guide to the history of diversity in the House of Commons

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Three former chancellors, Norman Lamont, Alistair Darling and George Osborne, in conversation on 'tax in the real world' at the Institute for Government, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Home Affairs Committee session on the work of the UK government's Home Office, with evidence from the home secretary Priti Patel and officials, from Wednesday 24 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the Booker prize-winning author Douglas Stuart. His novel, Shuggie Bain, centres on a boy growing up amid poverty, addiction and intolerance in Glasgow. There are deep parallels with his own life. How does he extract so much love from hardship? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 18 February.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Statement in the Assembly on updates to the Covid-19 response and social restrictions in Northern Ireland, with first minister Arlene Foster, from Tuesday 16 March

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: The Sporting L World
programme.desc: As part of BBC Sport's LGBT+ History Month, Clare Balding looks at what it means to be a gay woman in sport. From the outing of high profile athletes in the 1980s through to today's younger stars, she'll reveal how women have adapted and changed as they try to reclaim the word 'Lesbian'.

programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave and his friend Zombie Katie are working on a school project together about endangered animals, so George takes them on an expedition to the local park to see what they can find. There they discover the much-fabled Beast of Grimsby - and Dave must decide between a gold star at school and the future of the beast.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles and singer, actor and comedian Gary Wilmot, while the host is also joined by some Easter chicks

programme.title: Extreme E Live
programme.desc: The Desert X-Prix. Coverage of the final race in the opening round of the inaugural series, held in AlUla, Saudi Arabia. Commentary from Jennie Gow and Andrew Coley, with Layla Anna-Lee reporting from the pit lane

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his favourite dishes for anniversaries, date nights and romantic get-togethers. He starts with broad beans with parmesan and pan-fried sole, and ends with an exotic fruit pannacotta. Plus, chef and good friend Paul Ainsworth cooks a special dish for Raymond - lamb hotpot for two

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: A look back through the archives at the auditions that shocked the nation and surprised the judges, featuring the first appearances of contestants and future stars including Susan Boyle, Paul Potts and Stavros Flatley

programme.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (2005)
programme.desc: Penniless Charlie Bucket is one of five lucky children who find a golden ticket in a chocolate bar, winning a once-in-a-lifetime tour of eccentric recluse Willy Wonka's magical sweet company. Unfortunately, the other winners each face a grim fate when they start to misbehave. Tim Burton's children's fantasy, based on Roald Dahl's novel, starring Johnny Depp, Freddie Highmore, Helena Bonham Carter, and Deep Roy playing all the Oompa Loompas

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Jeff Banks, Michelle Ackerley, Andy Crane and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard returns with the celebrity version of game show as Jason Manford, Iwan Thomas and Debbie McGeetake answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local photographer wins an urban myth competition with his creation of the legend of the Wolf Hunter, and the tall tale unexpectedly gains a cult following. However, when a man is killed, Barnaby and Winter must investigate if this myth has become murderous reality

programme.title: 21 Jump Street (2012)
programme.desc: Cops Schmidt and Jenko hated each other when they were at school together, but have grown up to become friends. They are forced to relive the horrors of their adolescence when their captain decides their youthful appearance makes them the perfect undercover agents to crack a high-school drug ring. Comedy inspired by the 1980s TV series, with Jonah Hill and Channing Tatum

programme.title: For the Love of Britain
programme.desc: Julie Walters presents a guide to some of the most beautiful sights Sussex and Kent have to offer. programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: In order to make himself an object of worship, Fizz makes people believe he's an alien coming to share his wisdom.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that the whole neighbourhood has been flooded, when in fact he is just at the local swimming pool.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, on a construction site, thinks he's been hired for a medieval theatre show. Fizz and Weasel bump into Magoo at the top of the tower.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: When a rare spider bites a scientist, Scott must locate the anti-venom carrier drone in a dense rainforest. Guest starring Peter Davison

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A trip to the cinema to see a spooky film scares Bean half to death

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The bumbling blockhead is mistaken for an escaped convict and finds himself behind bars - where he soon makes himself at home while the real baddie has an awful time in the hands of Bean's landlady

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Iris isn't having the best day, so Mike decides to try and make her feel better by putting on a circus on the patio

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave and his friend Zombie Katie are working on a school project together about endangered animals, so George takes them on an expedition to the local park to see what they can find. There they discover the much-fabled Beast of Grimsby - and Dave must decide between a gold star at school and the future of the beast.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles and singer, actor and comedian Gary Wilmot, while the host is also joined by some Easter chicks

programme.title: Extreme E Live
programme.desc: The Desert X-Prix. Coverage of the final race in the opening round of the inaugural series, held in AlUla, Saudi Arabia. Commentary from Jennie Gow and Andrew Coley, with Layla Anna-Lee reporting from the pit lane

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his favourite dishes for anniversaries, date nights and romantic get-togethers. He starts with broad beans with parmesan and pan-fried sole, and ends with an exotic fruit pannacotta. Plus, chef and good friend Paul Ainsworth cooks a special dish for Raymond - lamb hotpot for two

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: A look back through the archives at the auditions that shocked the nation and surprised the judges, featuring the first appearances of contestants and future stars including Susan Boyle, Paul Potts and Stavros Flatley

programme.title: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (2005)
programme.desc: Penniless Charlie Bucket is one of five lucky children who find a golden ticket in a chocolate bar, winning a once-in-a-lifetime tour of eccentric recluse Willy Wonka's magical sweet company. Unfortunately, the other winners each face a grim fate when they start to misbehave. Tim Burton's children's fantasy, based on Roald Dahl's novel, starring Johnny Depp, Freddie Highmore, Helena Bonham Carter, and Deep Roy playing all the Oompa Loompas

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Jeff Banks, Michelle Ackerley, Andy Crane and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard returns with the celebrity version of game show as Jason Manford, Iwan Thomas and Debbie McGeetake answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local photographer wins an urban myth competition with his creation of the legend of the Wolf Hunter, and the tall tale unexpectedly gains a cult following. However, when a man is killed, Barnaby and Winter must investigate if this myth has become murderous reality

programme.title: 21 Jump Street (2012)
programme.desc: Cops Schmidt and Jenko hated each other when they were at school together, but have grown up to become friends. They are forced to relive the horrors of their adolescence when their captain decides their youthful appearance makes them the perfect undercover agents to crack a high-school drug ring. Comedy inspired by the 1980s TV series, with Jonah Hill and Channing Tatum

programme.desc: Richard E Grant marks the 100th anniversary of the first appearance in print of Agatha Christie's Belgian sleuth, exploring the life of the writer and the events that inspired the plots, characters and locations of her many literary creations. Animated comedy, with the voices of Steve Carell, Jason Segel, Russell Brand and Julie Andrews

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part two of two. Brian moves into his cousin Jasper's Hollywood apartment and tries to start a career in screenwriting. programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is awaiting his annual jury summons, but there is trouble ahead when becomes the foreman in a trial where Roger is the defendant. Meanwhile, Jeff and Hayley move in with the Smiths, and Francine tries to get Jeff to clean up his act

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Freight Train invites Cleveland to a lowrider convention, but his lack of amiability leads Donna to believe he is experiencing a mental breakdown. Meanwhile, Rallo and Cleveland Junior take a cherry picker from a building site and use it to carry out good deeds in the neighbourhood

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland and Donna become exhausted by the hectic schedules of their children, but problems arise when they enlist other family members to help them keep up. Meanwhile, Freight Train takes Cleveland Jr to the YMCA, where the youngster excels during a pole-dancing class

Horror, starring Devon Sawa, Ali Larter, Kerr Smith, Kristen Cloke and Seann William Scott

programme.desc: Night Angel, Astronaut, T-Rex and Rhino perform for guest judge Will Arnett and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three. Nick Cannon presents as one of the singers is unmasked and sent home

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a one-off 1980s edition of the dating game. A group of men enters the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)
programme.desc: The young wizard and his friends return for their second term at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry to discover a strange serpentine power is petrifying the other students. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson and Kenneth Branagh

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter discovers he has an African-American ancestor and his efforts to embrace his new-found heritage end with his demanding reparation from Lois's family. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to find out how cheerleaders control men's minds

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes the president of a cigarette company that uses toy products to promote smoking among children. Lois initially urges him to take a stand against the policy - but the perks of his new job soon persuade her to drop her opposition

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A hurricane sweeps through Quahog and damages the Drunken Clam - which is then bought at a knock-down price by an opportunistic expat Brit who angers the locals by turning it into an English-themed pub. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to teach the new landlord's three-year-old daughter how to speak proper English

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg tries to get friendly with the in-crowd at school, but becomes the target of their cruel mockery. Peter competes against Chris in a variety of winter pursuits, including a skating race and the unusual 'kitchenware on face and head' contest - but there is a surprise in store for him

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: The Smiths are forced to move into a hotel following an incident in which Roger released lethal gas into their home. However, Francine soon becomes bored with her new surroundings and decides to assume the identity of a businesswoman

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A battle between Stan and Buckle breaks out when they try to outdo each other with their Halloween decorations. Animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Scott Grimes

programme.desc: Maya Jama, Katherine Ryan and MNEK go up against Lady Leshurr, Judi Love and Ms Banks in rap battles, dance-offs & freestyling challenges. Jordan Stephens hosts

programme.title: Love Bites
programme.desc: Joining host Scarlett Moffatt is singleton James, who's choosing between Mads, Amy and Jess to date based solely on their cooking skills

programme.title: Love Bites
programme.desc: Single lads Cosmo, Charlie and Danny try to impress 22-year-old media performance graduate Brent with their cooking skills. Presented by Scarlett Moffatt

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Manager Rylan Clark-Neal invites Diversity's Jordan Banjo and Perri Kiely, pop icon Martin Kemp and DJ son Roman, and Gavin and Stacey stars Joanna Page and Melanie Walters into his supermarket for the chance to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show which takes place in public locations around the UK. He invites passers-by in Newcastle to take part in a series of unusual challenges, with a prize of £1,000 up for grabs

programme.title: Hop (2011)
programme.desc: The Easter bunny's son is about to take over the chocolate-delivering business, but rejects his responsibilities and heads for Hollywood to seek fame and fortune as a drummer. He makes life difficult for the directionless slacker who takes him in, but they must work together to avert a crisis on Easter island. Part-animated comedy, starring James Marsden, with the voice of Russell Brand

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire (2005)
programme.desc: Adolescent wizard Harry is unexpectedly chosen to represent Hogwarts School in a prestigious magical tournament, despite being legally too young to enter. Along with charming sixth-year student Cedric Diggory, Harry takes on the champions of two other magical academies visiting the school, facing deadly monsters in a series of death-defying challenges. Meanwhile, the followers of the dreaded Lord Voldemort plot to restore their master to the land of the living. Fourth instalment of the fantasy saga, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Robbie Coltrane, Ralph Fiennes, Michael Gambon, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson, Brendan Gleeson and Robert Pattinson

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois shows Meg how to have fun during a spring break at the beach, while Peter rashly takes out a loan and buys a boat, determined to try his hand at commercial fishing

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins visit Lois's pregnant sister and help deliver her baby, who catches them off guard by putting in a premature appearance. Exhilarated, they decide to try for another child of their own - but Stewie is horrified and tries every trick in the book to prevent conception

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and his friends go on an extended drinking binge in a bid to win a tour of the local brewery - but their bad behaviour lands them in trouble with owner Pawtucket Pat, who bears more than a passing resemblance to Willy Wonka. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter realises Chris is utterly irresponsible, and tries to rectify matters by getting him a job at the local golf course, hoping he will benefit from being entrusted with regular adult duties. Animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Seth Green

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is awarded a prestigious job as a sniffer dog for the Quahog police department, only to suffer the embarrassment of becoming addicted to cocaine and referred to a rehabilitation clinic

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: In the 100th episode of the animated comedy, Stan and Francine try to stop Hayley from eloping with her boyfriend by offering a $50,000 reward to anyone stopping them. The entire town takes up the offer, including Steve and Roger under the alias Wheels and the Legman. Meanwhile, tragedy strikes at Langley Falls when 100 characters are killed off

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan creates a clone of Steve, and takes the opportunity to challenge Francine to a parenting competition in which they each raise one of the teenagers to determine who has the better child-rearing skills. Meanwhile, Roger tries to find a missing bride and groom in Thailand

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: An outbreak of headlice at school forces Rallo to cut off his beloved Afro, and when the other kids make fun of him, Cleveland Jr steps in to look after his little brother. Meanwhile, Freight Train and Cookie leave Cleveland to house-sit for the weekend, but things go badly wrong when his friends visit

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna is annoyed when Cleveland and Cookie spend time with members of the Silver Sunset Society after they are mistaken for husband and wife. Meanwhile, Rallo steals chocolate from a street vendor, but tries to put the blame on Cleveland Junior when he is caught

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The final four cook for club goers who have taken over Hell's Kitchen. Dinner service gives each of the chefs a chance to run the pass, but Gordon is out to sabotage them

programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: The Flintstones (1994)
programme.desc: Blue-collar caveman Fred Flintstone is promoted to an executive position at work and unwittingly becomes embroiled in an embezzlement scheme - thanks in no small part to his seductive new assistant. Live-action version of the cartoon comedy, starring John Goodman, Rick Moranis, Rosie O'Donnell, Halle Berry and Elizabeth Taylor

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A police officer has questions, Dan gives Bob advice, and Hope worries he has overstepped the mark

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone accuses Fiz of shopping Alina to Immigration. One of Harvey's dealers, Ned, turns up at Leanne's flat and explains that he will be using the place to bag up drugs. Faye and Craig's plans for a romantic afternoon are ruined by Elaine

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone admits to Fiz that he is in love with Alina, Leanne meets up with the police and fills them in on Ned's drug plans, and Brian confronts Dev over stealing business from the Kabin

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Ellen talks to reality TV star Kris Jenner, who tells her all about Keeping Up with the Kardashians. Plus, Madison Beer performs Blue

programme.title: Love Bites
programme.desc: Scarlett Moffatt hosts, as singleton Rashaun chooses to date one of Harley, Naomi and Keelie - not on their looks but their cooking skills. After picking his favourite menu, the cook behind it reveals themselves and joins him for a date

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Night Angel, Astronaut, T-Rex and Rhino perform for guest judge Will Arnett and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three. Nick Cannon presents as one of the singers is unmasked and sent home

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a one-off 1980s edition of the dating game. A group of men enters the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)
programme.desc: The young wizard and his friends return for their second term at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry to discover a strange serpentine power is petrifying the other students. Meanwhile, Harry is haunted by a sinister voice in his head urging him to uncover the dark force that is stalking the corridors of the school. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson and Kenneth Branagh

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (2002)
programme.desc: The young wizard and his friends return for their second term at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry to discover a strange serpentine power is petrifying the other students. Meanwhile, Harry is haunted by a sinister voice in his head urging him to uncover the dark force that is stalking the corridors of the school. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Rupert Grint, Emma Watson and Kenneth Branagh

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The family visits Lois's parents, where Peter thinks he may finally be winning the approval of his father-in-law - until Brian falls in love with the Pewterschmidts' dog Sea Breeze and allows his heart to lead him into trouble

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter discovers he has an African-American ancestor and his efforts to embrace his new-found heritage end with his demanding reparation from Lois's family. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to find out how cheerleaders control men's minds

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes the president of a cigarette company that uses toy products to promote smoking among children. Lois initially urges him to take a stand against the policy - but the perks of his new job soon persuade her to drop her opposition

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A hurricane sweeps through Quahog and damages the Drunken Clam - which is then bought at a knock-down price by an opportunistic expat Brit who angers the locals by turning it into an English-themed pub. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to teach the new landlord's three-year-old daughter how to speak proper English

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg tries to get friendly with the in-crowd at school, but becomes the target of their cruel mockery. Peter competes against Chris in a variety of winter pursuits, including a skating race and the unusual 'kitchenware on face and head' contest - but there is a surprise in store for him

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: The Smiths are forced to move into a hotel following an incident in which Roger released lethal gas into their home. However, Francine soon becomes bored with her new surroundings and decides to assume the identity of a businesswoman

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A battle between Stan and Buckle breaks out when they try to outdo each other with their Halloween decorations. Animated comedy, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Scott Grimes

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, Katherine Ryan and MNEK go up against Lady Leshurr, Judi Love and Ms Banks in rap battles, dance-offs & freestyling challenges. Jordan Stephens hosts

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Comedy quiz hosted by Joel Dommett, with Joey Essex, Amber Davies, Arron Crascall, Frankie Bridge, AJ Odudu and Russell Kane making cold calls to businesses to try to get the answers to quiz questions and win money for members of the public

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by new team captain Scarlett Moffatt as three friends take on celebrities - Mark Francis, Harriet Kemsley, Lloyd Griffith and Snoochie Shy

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: More Incredible and peculiar record-breaking attempts, featuring Australian diving pigs, the most tattooed man on the planet and dogs removing socks from people's feet. Matt Edmonds narrates

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are chosen to go on a training course. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A 999 call is heard, Mandy confronts Paul, and Liv pieces everything together

programme.title: Carry On Cruising (1962)
programme.desc: The captain of a cruise ship bound for the Mediterranean is less than impressed when five of his regular shipmates are replaced at the last minute. Despite their best efforts to earn his respect and an eagerness to please, the new recruits seem doomed to cause chaos whatever they do. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Liz Fraser and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Carry On Screaming (1966)
programme.desc: A sinister boffin, his sultry sister and two genetic misfits kidnap innocent women from spooky Hocomb Woods and turn them into mannequins - but two dim-witted detectives look set to throw a spanner in the works. Comedy horror, starring Kenneth Williams, Fenella Fielding, Harry H Corbett, Charles Hawtrey, Jim Dale, Joan Sims and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Carry On Again Doctor (1969)
programme.desc: A disgraced surgeon sets up a clinic in the hope of promoting a supposedly miraculous slimming potion made from secret ingredients discovered on a remote tropical island. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jim Dale, Barbara Windsor, Charles Hawtrey, Hattie Jacques, Pat Coombs and Joan Sims

programme.title: Carry On Follow That Camel (1967)
programme.desc: A disgraced English gentleman joins the Foreign Legion to restore his honour, and proves himself an unlikely hero. Meanwhile, his naive lost love learns he was innocent, and heads to the desert to bring him home. Comedy, starring Phil Silvers, Jim Dale, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Peter Butterworth, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims

programme.title: Carry On Matron (1972)
programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Carry On Abroad (1972)
programme.desc: British holiday-makers jet off on a package holiday to the Spanish resort of Elsbels. Unluckily, not only is the hotel short of staff, but it also has no walls, ceilings or entertainment. Further mishaps leave the tourists fuming - until they sample a local punch laced with an aphrodisiac. Comedy, with Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna meets the Maharaja of Dungarpur who shows her around his lakeside palace, and a Dalit community in Gujarat, considered to be India's lowest caste. Braving the roads of Mumbai, Joanna takes a ride in the city's only all-female taxi company and visits the Times of India, where her uncle was editor of the paper in the 1930s and 40s. She then overcomes her fear of heights to explore the World One Tower, soon to be Mumbai's tallest luxury residential building

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. In the first of this two-part story, suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A 999 call is heard, Mandy confronts Paul, and Liv pieces everything together

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dismisses Jean's interest in the daily horoscopes as complete nonsense - but his opinion changes when they start coming true

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne starts work at the Kabin, eager to please Rita. Samantha is sick of Des hanging around her. Hayley thanks Alma for her support. Les has an accident at the Red Rec

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is embarrassed when Hayley buys her an expensive sweater. Samantha visits Ritchie and asks him not to divorce her. Gail receives a shock from Leanne

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The rest of the Dingle family arrive for Belle's christening. Donna and Andy talk about their troubles. Scott attempts to make Kelly jealous by flirting with Tricia.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ashley confronts Lisa about the missing church money. Sean Reynolds fleeces Chris out of a haulage contract. Scott lies about what happened between him and Tricia.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Carry On Jack (1963)
programme.desc: A bumbling midshipman's uniform is stolen by a woman desperate to go to sea, who adopts his identity. Later, the hapless sailor is press-ganged on to the same ship as her. However, while trying to prove who he really is, he has to contend with mutinies, pirates and the Spanish Navy. Comedy, with Bernard Cribbins, Juliet Mills, Kenneth Williams and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Carry On Cleo (1964)
programme.desc: A captured Briton is mistaken for a heroic swordsman and promoted to the Roman Royal Guard, with orders to protect Julius Caesar. But the last thing he and the fated emperor expect is an assassination attempt from within Caesar's own circle of friends. Comedy, starring Amanda Barrie, Kenneth Williams, Sid James, Kenneth Connor, Jim Dale, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Carry On Henry (1971)
programme.desc: King Henry VIII plots to rid himself of his new French wife when her craving for garlic becomes too much to cope with. However, he cannot afford to anger the king of France, and tries to make it look as if his queen has had an affair. Comedy, starring Sid James, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Terry Scott, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Carry On Up the Jungle (1970)
programme.desc: A Victorian explorer recalls how he was recruited by an aristocrat to find her missing son in the African jungle. Teaming up with a fearless hunter, he hoped to find a rare bird on his journey - but instead fell into the clutches of an all-female tribe. Comedy, starring Sid James, Frankie Howerd, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Terry Scott

programme.title: Carry On at Your Convenience (1971)
programme.desc: Production at toilet manufacturers WC Boggs is brought to a halt when an over-eager trade union representative calls a strike. However, worker unrest is quickly quelled by a jolly trip to the seaside. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Cope, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Carry On Up the Khyber (1968)
programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd as the Cornish copper. When a bookshop owner is murdered in Penzance, the detective discovers all is not harmonious between the members of one of the area's most prominent families. With Kevin Quarmby and Carla Mendonca

programme.title: Carry On Cowboy (1966)
programme.desc: Notorious outlaw the Rumpo Kid runs riot in lawless Stodge City. A local judge sends for someone to clean up the town - a summons taken literally by a timid English sanitation expert who becomes the new sheriff. Comedy Western, starring Jim Dale, Sid James, Charles Hawtrey, Kenneth Williams, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Donna and Kelly try to find Andy in Leeds. Zak hatches a plan to get the church money back. Scott tries to convince Roy that Kelly isn't the right girl for him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon schemes to get Biff to admit he fancies Kathy. Zak, Butch and Mandy attempt to dupe some American tourists. Chris discovers more of Reynolds' underhand tactics

programme.title: Carry On Girls (1973)
programme.desc: The council of a seaside town is in dire need of a means to attract tourists to its resort, so announces a beauty contest. While the conniving organiser plots a series of publicity stunts, an outraged feminist group plans to sabotage the event. Comedy, starring Sid James, June Whitfield, Joan Sims, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw and Peter Butterworth

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike bring home a tramp, much to Sid's disgust. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Suzy gives Stan food from the canteen to take home - but it's stolen property. When Blakey discovers that Stan and Jack are biting the hand that feeds them, he vows to make trouble. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd as the Cornish copper. When a bookshop owner is murdered in Penzance, the detective discovers all is not harmonious between the members of one of the area's most prominent families. With Kevin Quarmby and Carla Mendonca

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate seeks Nick's advice about one of her patients, who seems to be receiving threats, and Greengrass tries to take the law into his own hands. Dora Bryan guest stars

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy invites Tricia to the Vet's Ball. Andy is allowed to stay with the Sugdens, who plan to adopt him. Zak gives Ashley the stolen money back. Roy pesters Kelly to marry him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Donna worries about Viv's reaction to her news. Marlon schemes to get Biff and Kathy together. Scott almost blurts out Kelly's secret to Roy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is shocked to hear Roy tell her that he is seeing Hayley. Gail is horrified when Nick moves his things out. Maxine feels awful for getting Fiona into trouble

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne and Nick enjoy the party Les and Janice throw for them. Des confronts Ritchie in London. Roy and Hayley find themselves stuck in a tunnel at the protest

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin

Comedy, starring Sid James, Frankie Howerd, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Terry Scott

programme.title: Carry On at Your Convenience (1971)
programme.desc: Production at toilet manufacturers WC Boggs is brought to a halt when an over-eager trade union representative calls a strike. However, worker unrest is quickly quelled by a jolly trip to the seaside. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Cope, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Carry On Up the Khyber (1968)
programme.desc: British forces in colonial India find themselves in deep trouble when a manic local ruler discovers the soldiers' fearsome reputation is thoroughly undeserved - prompting open rebellion. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Roy Castle, Joan Sims, Bernard Bresslaw and Terry Scott

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd as the Cornish copper. When a bookshop owner is murdered in Penzance, the detective discovers all is not harmonious between the members of one of the area's most prominent families. With Kevin Quarmby and Carla Mendonca

programme.title: Carry On Cowboy (1966)
programme.desc: Notorious outlaw the Rumpo Kid runs riot in lawless Stodge City. A local judge sends for someone to clean up the town - a summons taken literally by a timid English sanitation expert who becomes the new sheriff. Comedy Western, starring Jim Dale, Sid James, Charles Hawtrey, Kenneth Williams, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alma is shocked to hear Roy tell her that he is seeing Hayley. Gail is horrified when Nick moves his things out. Maxine feels awful for getting Fiona into trouble

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne and Nick enjoy the party Les and Janice throw for them. Des confronts Ritchie in London. Roy and Hayley find themselves stuck in a tunnel at the protest

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious shooting of archaeology student Celia Dawe and the significance of matching playing cards lead Wycliffe on a manhunt across Cornwall. Police drama based on the novel by WJ Burley, guest starring Bill Nighy alongside Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The detective discovers his half-sister's daughter has committed suicide and sets out to find what prompted it, entering the world of teenage rave parties and drug-taking. Meanwhile, Lewis has his sights set on promotion and studies for exams. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Sorcha Cusack, Jason Isaacs and John Junkin

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Conclusion. Despite his reservations, Hornblower is reluctant to dishonour his bride-to-be by leaving her standing at the altar. However, with the ink barely dry on the marriage certificate, a surprise guest at the wedding breakfast despatches the groom to France on a mission to uncover the whereabouts of a missing sloop and its captain, Bracegirdle. En route, Hornblower rescues two strangers caught in a storm. Historical Navy drama, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay, Paul McGann and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: The Guns of Navarone (1961)

programme.title: Casino Royale (2006)
programme.desc: James Bond achieves his '00' status and is dispatched on the trail of Le Chiffre, an international criminal financing terrorist organisations around the world. Bond's investigation leads to a casino in Montenegro, where he takes part in a high-stakes poker game in a bid to bankrupt Le Chiffre and put an end to his plans. Spy thriller, directed by Martin Campbell and starring Daniel Craig in his first outing as 007, with Eva Green, Mads Mikkelsen, Judi Dench and Giancarlo Giannini

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth and the Spy in the Palace
programme.desc: The story of Anthony Blunt, royal appointed art expert and KGB spy, who worked as Surveyor of the Queen's Pictures for 27 years. The programme examines how he was previously employed by George VI to loot art treasures from Germany in the aftermath of the Second World War, and that despite Blunt's protestations of loyalty to the monarchy, classified documents have since revealed that he provided damaging information about the royal family to his Soviet employers

On day two, Will and Kim set out to surprise their guests, and no napkins, massive portions and an overpowering dessert certainly raise eyebrows. On the last night, Pasha and Haleh are expecting their Iranian feast to leave the competition in their wake. However, problems arise when Bob reveals his dislike of their key ingredient of lamb, jeopardising their chances of winning the £1,000 prize

programme.desc: Phoebe's big day arrives, but wedding planner Monica and stand-in father of the bride Joey take their roles a little too seriously. To make matters worse, a blizzard hits New York and one of Mike's ushers is indisposed, forcing Ross and Chandler to step into the breach
